_meta.purpose: Human-curated watchlist of GitHub 'refuge' repos (mirrors of censored material) plus GitHub's own transparency repos, for collectors/github_refuge.py. Evidence-bound, like zh_censorship_gazetteer.json.
_meta.note: PUBLIC events / public sources ONLY. We watch the PRESSURE metadata (takedown notices, status changes, fork/star bursts), never the censored content itself and never personal data about a maintainer. Human-authored; never delegated to a model.
_meta.safety: INERT BY DEFAULT. `active_watchlist` is intentionally EMPTY so the collector makes zero network calls out of the box. A deployer copies entries from `documented_repos` into `active_watchlist` to activate, and injects a read-only fetch + governance (kill switch + rate ceiling under 60 req/hr/IP).
_meta.schema: full_name, terms(evidence bindings -> DDTI terms), evidence(event+date+source), pressure_history

transparency_repos.role: primary DMCA takedown feed (Markdown, by date)
government takedown notices; renamed after a China request 2016-06-09

_meta.complainants_note: Known censor-aligned complainant names are matched lexically in notice text; see collectors/github_refuge.DEFAULT_COMPLAINANTS.

evidence.event: anti-996 overwork protest; most-starred repo on GitHub within ~72h; domain blocked in-app by Tencent/Qihoo 360/Alipay/WeChat while the repo stayed up
COVID outbreak reports/diaries archive; maintainers set it private to reduce risk
'resist 404' censored-article archive; three contributors detained April 2020 ('picking quarrels'); reachable abroad but GFW-blocked while github.com stays up

pressure_history: selective GFW block of the specific repo

evidence.event: crowd-sourced database of CCP elite family wealth; the pseudonymous author was detained in 2021
